title: Korriban zombie
content:
{{Top|ffa|canon=Animated skeleton}}
{{Youmay|the [[Zombie/Legends|undead]] originally created by [[Dathka Graush]] on [[Korriban/Legends|Korriban]]|the similar [[Sith undead]] that resided on [[Dromund Kaas/Legends|Dromund Kaas]]|linebreak=1}}
{{Species
|image=[[File:Korriban_zombie.jpg]]
|name=Korriban zombie
|designation=[[Sentience/Legends|Semi-sentient]]<ref name="Wizards">{{Wizards|url=default.asp?x=starwars/article/sw20031002korriban|text=Korriban: Planet of Lost Souls|int=Korriban: Planet of Lost Souls}}</ref>

|haircolor=Variable to none<ref name="TotJ3" />
|feathers=
|eyecolor=Variable to none<ref name="Wizards" /><ref name="Showdown" /><ref name="TotJ3" />
|distinctions=*[[Zombie/Legends|Undead]]<ref name="Wizards" /><ref name="Showdown" />

{{Quote|This is the Sith's greeting for ''tomb robbers''—magic and machines created them when the tombs were ''built''!|The spirit of Freedon Nadd|<ref name="TotJ3" />}}
'''Korriban zombies''', also known as '''Korriban Sith zombies''', were [[Sentience/Legends|semi-sentient]], [[Zombie/Legends|undead]] [[Humanoid/Legends|humanoid]] predators native to the [[Sith/Legends|Sith]] [[Planet/Legends|tombworld]] [[Korriban/Legends|Korriban]]. Originally created prior to [[7000 BBY]] by [[Sith (species)/Legends|Sith]] [[King/Legends|King]] [[Dathka Graush]]'s [[Tsaiwinokka Hoyakut|experiments]] using [[Sith alchemy/Legends|Sith alchemy]] and [[Sith sorcery/Legends|magic]] to reanimate corpses, the creatures were soon discovered to be capable of creating more of their kind by biting living sentient beings. After a tumultuous fifty-[[Standard year/Legends|year]] reign, Graush was [[Death/Legends|assassinated]], and the spirits animating the zombies were collected in a [[Heart of Graush|crystal]] that was buried with him in [[Dathka Graush's tomb|his tomb]] located in Korriban's [[Valley of Golg]].

For the next seven millennia, the zombies served as guardians of the tombs of the [[Sith Lord/Legends|Sith Lords]] in both the Valley of Golg and the [[Valley of the Dark Lords/Legends|Valley of the Dark Lords]]. The creatures laid dormant for extended periods of time, and were only awakened by the commands of the deceased Sith [[Lord/Legends|Lords]]' [[Force ghost/Legends|spirits]] or the disturbance of Graush's [[Crystal/Legends|crystal]] in order to eliminate any intruders or would-be tomb robbers. The zombies' limited intelligence allowed them to wield weapons, and this attribute, coupled with their intimidating presence, ensured that few sentients prevailed during an encounter with the creatures. In [[3997 BBY]], self-declared [[Jedi Knight/Legends|Jedi Knight]] [[Exar Kun/Legends|Exar Kun]] survived an encounter with the guardian zombies while exploring the Valley of the [[Dark Lord of the Sith/Legends|Dark Lords]], as did a group of [[Jedi/Legends|Jedi]] Knights and their respective [[Padawan/Legends|Padawans]] nearly four millennia after Kun, during their pursuit of a [[The galaxy/Legends|galactic]] [[Terrorist/Legends|criminal]]. A subsequent encounter with the creatures occurred six [[Standard month/Legends|months]] before the [[Battle of Yavin/Legends|Battle of Yavin]], when a [[Pirate/Legends|pirate]] [[Jynni's Virtue|vessel]] crash-landed near the Valley of Golg.

==Biology and appearance==
{{Quote|The dead walk—|Babbnod Luroon|<ref name="Wizards" />}}
[[Korriban/Legends|Korriban]] [[Zombie/Legends|zombies]], also called Korriban [[Sith/Legends|Sith]] zombies, referred to the name given to the first corpses of [[Sentience/Legends|sentient]] beings that had been reanimated after [[Death/Legends|death]] by<ref name="Wizards" /> [[Sith (species)/Legends|Sith]] [[King/Legends|King]]<ref name="Mindor">''[[Luke Skywalker and the Shadows of Mindor]]''</ref> [[Dathka Graush]]'s experiments with [[Sith alchemy/Legends|Sith alchemy]] and [[Sith sorcery/Legends|magic]].<ref name="Wizards" /><ref name="CSWE" /> At some later point, mechanical techniques were added to the process.<ref name="TotJ3" /> As [[Time/Legends|time]] progressed, it became apparent that these [[Humanoid/Legends|humanoids]] were capable of creating more of their kind through biting their victims.<ref name="Wizards" /><ref name="CSWE" /> These wounds became infected with Sith alchemical elements that would induce transformation into the undead.<ref name="Wizards" />

The zombies' physical appearances varied widely,<ref name="TotJ3" /><ref name="Showdown" /> depending on their former [[Species/Legends|species]] and [[Gender/Legends|gender]]. Moreover, their original features were distorted depending on damage sustained during battle and by the ravages of time, as the zombies were capable of laying dormant for extended durations. There had been accounts of beings who had been felled during battle and had risen almost immediately with missing [[Limb/Legends|limbs]] and massive gaping wounds.<ref name="Wizards" /> Others had observed that the zombies were little more than half-rotted husks that emanated a horrid stench and moved with an odd shuffling gait.<ref name="Showdown" />

With the metamorphosis into a zombie, the creature gained heightened [[Eye/Legends|visual]] acuity in the dark and strength superior to that of living beings. Furthermore, it was impossible to halt a zombie's advance with normal physical damage. The only ways of destroying a zombie was massive trauma to the head, such as [[Blaster/Legends|blaster]] fire,<ref name="Wizards" /> or severing the head from the rest of the body.<ref name="CSWE" />

==Behavior and intelligence==
{{Quote|Obviously, the ''guardian spirits'' of this world can't tell the difference between friend and foe!|Exar Kun|<ref name="TotJ3" />}}
The process of transforming into a zombie negated the impact of former attributes—except in retaining some basic skills, such as weapons aptitude—thus, reducing a sentient being into a semi-sentient predatory creature. The creature was left with an overwhelming need to feed on the flesh of living sentient beings,<ref name="Wizards" /> although the zombie was able to dampen its appetite with bodies buried in the tombs. When presented with the opportunity to feed on living flesh, a zombie worked relentlessly to make the kill. Its limited intelligence allowed it to wield [[Blaster pistol/Legends|blaster pistols]] and [[Thermal detonator/Legends|thermal detonators]],<ref name="Showdown" /> among other weapons, depending on its former species. Quite often, the intended victim was paralyzed with fear due to the zombie's terrifying presence, thus inadvertently facilitating his or her own death.<ref name="Wizards" />

==History==
===Origin===
{{Quote|Given the illusion of life, the dead seek only to pull others down into the grave.|[[Sorzus Syn]], extract from [[Sorzus Syn's chronicle|Chronicle of Sorzus Syn]]|<ref name="SW:BTS">''[[Book of Sith: Secrets from the Dark Side]]''</ref>}}
[[File:Tsaiwinokka_hoyakut-BOS.png|thumb|250px|Sith undead could be created through [[Sith necromancy|necromancy]].]]
The Sith King Dathka Graush and his forces were the victors in one of [[Korriban/Legends|Korriban's]] cyclical civil wars, and during his fifty-year reign over Korriban—one that the Sith considered to be full of terror and cruelty, even by their own standards—a substantial amount of corpses accumulated. Due in part to the sheer amount of raw material available to him, Graush's experiments with Sith alchemy and magic led to his discovery of a method of reanimating these corpses,<ref name="Wizards" /> ''[[Tsaiwinokka Hoyakut]]'' spell, translated from ancient Sith as the "Reanimated Dead,"<ref name="SW:BTS" /> thus forming a zombie [[Army/Legends|army]].<ref name="Wizards" /> Furthermore, during this time, Graush, using Sith magic and the most advanced technologies, had his [[Heart/Legends|heart]] replaced with a [[Dark side of the Force/Legends|dark side]],<ref name="CSWE:Heart">''[[The Complete Star Wars Encyclopedia]]'' {{C|Under ''Heart of Graush''}}</ref> [[Force-sensitive/Legends|Force-sensitive]] [[Heart of Graush|crystal]]<ref name="Wizards" /> jewel that was imbued with over a thousand [[Force ghost/Legends|Sith spirits]].<ref name="CSWE:Heart" /> After Graush was later [[Assassin/Legends|assassinated]], the spirits of his zombie army were captured in his heart,<ref name="CSWE" /> and the [[Crystal/Legends|crystal]] was buried along with Graush's mummified remains in [[Dathka Graush's tomb|his tomb]] in Korriban's [[Valley of Golg]].<ref name="Wizards" /> The zombies laid dormant for extended periods of time, only awakening when a being touched Graush's heart, releasing their spirits. The spirits then searched for their former bodies and new victims to infect.<ref name="CSWE" />

===Guardian of the tombs===
{{Quote|All those who trespass here are cursed to guard this place forever.|The spirit of Freedon Nadd|<ref name="TotJaudio">[[Tales of the Jedi: Dark Lords of the Sith (audio drama)|''Tales of the Jedi: Dark Lords of the Sith'' audio drama]]</ref>}}
[[File:Zombiecorpses.jpg|thumb|left|250px|Exar Kun comes upon the remains of long-dead space pirates.]]
[[3997 BBY|Three millennia later]],<ref name="TotJ2">[[Tales of the Jedi – Dark Lords of the Sith 2|''Tales of the Jedi – Dark Lords of the Sith'' 2]]</ref> [[Jedi Knight/Legends|Jedi Knight]] [[Exar Kun/Legends|Exar Kun]]—under guidance from the spirit of deceased [[Dark Lord of the Sith/Legends|Dark Lord]] [[Freedon Nadd]]—arrived on Korriban during his search for the knowledge and power possessed by the ancient [[Sith Lord/Legends|Sith Lords]]. Kun's explorations of the [[Planet/Legends|planet]] led him to the [[Valley of the Dark Lords/Legends|Valley of the Dark Lords]]<ref name="EmpireEnd">[[Empire's End 2|''Empire's End'' 2]]</ref>—a valley of burial tombs and temples—where he came upon scattered [[Bone/Legends|bones]],<ref name="TotJaudio" /> the wreckage of a [[Starship/Legends|starship]], and [[Skeleton/Legends|skeletons]] of long-dead [[Realspace/Legends|space]] [[Pirate/Legends|pirates]]; Kun surmised that he had found the remnants of tomb robbers.<ref name="TotJ3" /> Nadd's spirit appeared at Kun's side and disclosed that Kun was seeing the remains of those who violated the will of the Dark [[Lord/Legends|Lords]] and their resting place. The spirit further explained that any being entering the domain of the Dark Lords was doomed to remain.<ref name="TotJaudio" /> Moments later, dark side energies—created by magic and machines at the time of the building of the tombs—whirled around the corpses, reanimating them.<ref name="TotJ3" /> The zombies advanced on Kun, calling him a defiler and stating that they were the guardians of the tombs. As their attack began in earnest, the zombies claimed that no one was allowed to interrupt the sleep of the Dark Lords of the Sith.<ref name="TotJaudio" /> Nadd's spirit counseled Kun to take refuge in the [[Great Temple (Korriban)|Great Temple]] located nearby to escape the zombies and continue his investigation.<ref name="TotJ3" />

In the ensuing millennia, vague rumors passed amongst [[The galaxy/Legends|galactic]] [[Spacer/Legends|spacers]] claiming that Sith alchemy could be used to reanimate corpses, and that Korriban zombies occasionally dispatched unlucky [[Freighter/Legends|freighter]] crews.<ref name="Atlas">''[[The Essential Atlas]]'' {{C|Under ''Korriban''}}</ref> A group of four [[Jedi/Legends|Jedi]] Knights and their respective [[Padawan/Legends|Padawans]], which included [[Obi-Wan Kenobi/Legends|Obi-Wan Kenobi]] and [[Anakin Skywalker/Legends|Anakin Skywalker]], were forced to battle Korriban zombies during [[Mission to Korriban (23 BBY)|their pursuit]] of galactic [[Terrorist/Legends|criminal]] [[Granta Omega]]. Due in part to his obsession with the Sith<ref name="Omega">{{Wizards|url=default.asp?x=starwars/article/sw20030612omega|text=Granta Omega: An Expanded Universe Character}}</ref> and his hatred of the Jedi, Omega had obtained the support of a mysterious Sith Lord in his endeavors to destroy the [[Jedi Order/Legends|Jedi Order]] and wreak havoc within the [[Galactic Republic/Legends|Galactic Republic]]. Once the connection between the two had been discovered by the Jedi, Omega traveled to the Valley of the Dark Lords to meet with his Sith benefactor, the Jedi in pursuit. Having tracked Omega to one of the [[Burial/Legends|tombs]], Kenobi and Skywalker, at the forefront of the search, plunged deeper into the tomb while the other Jedi were held up near the entrance. Soon, the two were assaulted by a horrific stench mere moments before the Korriban zombies lurched out of the depths of the tomb and charged. Skywalker had been momentarily stunned by the appearance of the zombies, having read about the undead but never expecting to encounter them. The Sith Lord had reawakened the zombies to guard the tombs—considered to be sacred Sith ground—and to defend Omega and his partner in crime, [[Jenna Zan Arbor]]. The creatures attacked the Jedi viciously with blasters and detonators. Kenobi and Skywalker made their way determinedly through the undead, deflecting blaster fire with their [[Lightsaber/Legends|lightsabers]], avoiding the detonators, and eliminating the reanimated corpses in their path, and continued their chase for Omega while the other Jedi destroyed the remaining zombies.<ref name="Showdown" />

===Reawakened master===
{{Quote|Wake up, there is work to do.|translated=1|The spirit of Dathka Graush|<ref name="Wizards" />}}
Sometime after the year [[11 ABY/Legends|11 ABY]],<ref>''[[The Complete Star Wars Encyclopedia]]'' {{C|Under ''Timeline of Galactic Events''}}. This date is based on the founding of the first [[Jedi Praxeum]] on [[Yavin 4/Legends|Yavin 4]] by [[Luke Skywalker/Legends|Luke Skywalker]]. ''[[Korriban: Planet of Lost Souls]]'' states that Skywalker sent out Jedi Academy researchers; therefore, it must have been after the founding of the first Academy.</ref> [[Jedi Master/Legends|Jedi Master]] [[Luke Skywalker/Legends|Luke Skywalker]]—founder of the [[New Jedi Order]] during the time of the [[New Republic/Legends|New Republic]]—sent [[Jedi historian|Jedi researchers]] to investigate the ancient Sith [[Homeworld/Legends|homeworld]] of Korriban. While searching the tomb of Dathka Graush, the researchers discovered a set of recorded logs belonging to a missing pirate freighter named ''[[Jynni's Virtue]]'' that identified a subsequent encounter with Korriban zombies. According to the logs—the time codes on the logs indicating that the first recording had been made exactly six [[Standard month/Legends|months]] before the [[Battle of Yavin/Legends|Battle of Yavin]]—the freighter had been disabled by an [[Galactic Empire/Legends|Imperial]] [[Scout ship/Legends|scout]] patrol and had been forced to make a crash-landing on the planet, near the Valley of Golg. The [[Captain/Legends|captain]] of ''Jynni's Virtue'', [[Naz Felyood]], explored the valley in search of treasure to plunder and came across Graush's tomb. Determining that Graush's [[Sith sarcophagus|sarcophagus]] most likely contained great treasure, the Captain removed the Heart of Graush from its resting place. Felyood became [[Curse of Graush|possessed]] by the spirit of Dathka Graush, thereby reawakening the zombie army that was now under his control. Felyood sent the undead to lay siege to the ''Virtue'', and, soon after, [[First Mate/Legends|First Mate]] [[Babbnod Luroon]] realized that she and the remainder of the crew were unlikely to survive. Luroon ordered [[Deksi Fivvl]], the ship's [[Engineer/Legends|engineer]], to rig the ''Virtue''{{'s}} [[Engine/Legends|engines]] to blow up the freighter. The resulting explosion destroyed the majority of the zombies laying siege to ''Jynni's Virtue'', as well as disintegrating the vessel and crew.<ref name="Wizards" />

In addition to finding the recordings, the Jedi researchers found a [[Last words of Captain Naz Felyood|brief hand-written message]] believed to be penned by Naz Felyood. In the letter, Felyood recounted the destruction of the ''Virtue'' and his subsequent loss of the Heart of Graush. As such, he was no longer possessed by the spirit of Graush;<ref name="Wizards" /> however, he was cursed to wander Korriban, forever immortal, in search of the jewel.<ref name="CSWE:Heart" /> The researchers were unable to find any evidence of the Heart or the zombie army and considered the return of Graush's zombie army a threat to anyone who would have landed on the planet. Many also considered it fortunate that [[Galactic Emperor/Legends|Emperor]] [[Palpatine/Legends|Palpatine]]—the Sith Lord ruler of the Galactic Empire—had never discovered the secrets to creating or reawakening the undead, thereby leaving the zombies confined to Korriban.<ref name="Wizards" />

==Behind the scenes==
===Notable appearances and development===
Korriban zombies made their first appearance in the [[Tales of the Jedi – Dark Lords of the Sith 3|''Tales of the Jedi – Dark Lords of the Sith'' 3]] [[Star Wars comic books|comic]], the third issue of the [[Star Wars: Tales of the Jedi – Dark Lords of the Sith|''Star Wars: Tales of the Jedi - Dark Lords of the Sith'']] story arc, published by [[Dark Horse Comics]] in [[1994]]. The zombies were not specifically named in this issue, however; they were referred to only as guardian spirits. In the [[Tales of the Jedi – Dark Lords of the Sith 4|fourth issue]] of the ''Dark Lords of the Sith'' story arc, published in early [[1995]], the zombies only received a mention as guardian creatures in the introductory synopsis. The release of the ''Dark Lords of the Sith'' [[Tales of the Jedi: Dark Lords of the Sith (audio drama)|audio drama]] in [[2005]] reinforced the zombies' role as guardians.

The third and fourth issues of the ''Dark Lords of the Sith'' story arc did not originally state the exact location of where Exar Kun had encountered the zombies. With the release of the [[Empire's End 2|''Empire's End'' 2]] comic in late 1995, the location was established; the caption of an image depicting the Great Temple—seen in both ''Empire's End 2'' and ''Descent to the Dark Side''—stated that the temple was located in the Valley of the Dark Lords. In [[2008]], ''[[The Complete Star Wars Encyclopedia]]'' identified the guardian creatures of the ''Dark Lords of the Sith'' as Korriban zombies by publishing an image of one of the ''Dark Lords of the Sith'' creatures under the Korriban zombie entry.

In [[2003]], the [[Wizards of the Coast]] ''[[Planet Hoppers]]'' [[Star Wars Roleplaying Game|roleplaying]] web supplement ''[[Korriban: Planet of Lost Souls]]'' first identified Korriban zombies by name. In addition to presenting detailed characteristics and a history of the zombies, this supplement also provided ''Korriban Sith zombie'' as an alternate name for the creatures. The zombies made their final appearance to date in [[2004]]'s ''[[Jedi Quest: The Final Showdown]]'', the last novel of the [[Star Wars: Jedi Quest|''Jedi Quest'']] young reader series. In [[2009]], the zombies were mentioned in ''[[The Essential Atlas]]'', under the Korriban planet entry.

===Inconsistencies===
A continuity inconsistency seems to exist concerning the origin of the Korriban zombies that has not been explained as of yet in ''[[Star Wars]]'' [[canon]]. ''Korriban: Planet of Lost Souls'' and ''The Complete Star Wars Encyclopedia'' both state that the zombies originated in the Valley of Golg, one of Korriban's many burial valleys. Furthermore, these two sources claim that the zombies were under the control of the Heart of Graush, located in the Valley of Golg, and would be awakened only when this jewel was disturbed. However, ''Dark Lords of the Sith'' and ''Jedi Quest'' place the zombies in Korriban's Valley of the Dark Lords, without any mention of being under the control of the Heart of Graush.
